title: CPU Structure, Pipeline, Branch Prediction, RISC and Parallel Architectures
subtopics: CPU structure (ALU, registers, control unit, PC, IR, MAR, MDR)

definitions.description: A technique for executing instructions in overlapping stages; an n-stage pipeline can have n instructions active simultaneously, one per stage.
The number of clock cycles needed to execute a single instruction (= number of pipeline stages).
The number of instructions completed per clock cycle; ideally 1 for a pipeline, less in practice due to dependencies.
A pipeline bubble inserted when an instruction must wait for a dependency to resolve; effectively a NOP is injected.
A hardware technique that routes a computed result directly from the ALU output to the input of a dependent instruction, reducing stalls.
A processor with multiple ALUs that can execute more than one instruction per clock cycle.
A processor with two or more independent pipelines capable of executing different instructions simultaneously.
A cache-like structure storing the recent branch history and target addresses, used for dynamic branch prediction.
CPU design philosophy: fewer, simpler instructions; large register file; fixed instruction format; load/store architecture.
CPU design philosophy: many complex instructions; small register file; variable instruction format.
Symmetric multiprocessing: all processors share one memory with equal access time.
Memory is physically distributed; access time depends on which processor accesses which memory module.

examples.name: Instruction execution steps (14 steps)
examples.description: Detailed breakdown from PC→MAR through write-back result to memory/register.

examples.name: Pipeline evolution in Intel processors
examples.description: Pentium III: 10 stages; Pentium IV Northwood: 20; Pentium IV Prescott: 32; AMD Athlon: 17.

examples.name: Data dependency in assembly
examples.description: 'mov eax,7; sub eax,3' – second instruction must wait for eax to be written by the first.

learning_objectives: Describe the internal structure of a CPU
List the steps of instruction execution
Explain how pipeline improves CPU throughput
Identify and resolve structural, data, and control pipeline dependencies
Compare stall and forwarding as dependency solutions
Explain static and dynamic branch prediction schemes
Compare RISC and CISC design philosophies
Describe multiprocessor memory architectures (UMA, NUMA, message-passing)
